page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 382,376.39 and a Passmark CPU score of 107,019.88. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 729.18 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 327.45 GB/sec for write operations. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database and caching scenarios, achieving 506.95 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and top-tier performance in Passmark database operations. Single-core performance remains in the average tier.

Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency due to its ARM-based architecture and integrated high-capacity NVMe storage. It is designed for high-throughput databases, large-scale static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and memory-intensive caching layers.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of system memory, 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score of 382,376.39 and high-speed Redis SET operations. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capability and high-capacity local storage offer excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency. This instance is highly suited for high-throughput databases, large-scale web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and enterprise caching workloads.
